text: Supervised learning is one of the most common types of machine learning. In this approach, a computer learns from training data, which consists of many labeled examples. For instance, if we want to teach a model to recognize cats in photos, we show it thousands of cat and non-cat images, each correctly labeled. The model studies these examples and learns patterns. Once trained, the model can make predictions on new, unseen images. To check its performance, we test its accuracy—how often its predictions are correct. Improving accuracy often requires more training data or better tuning of the model.
